info.title: Wit Agent Coordination Protocol
info.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol for coordinating multiple AI agents working on the same codebase. Transported over Unix domain socket at .wit/daemon.sock via HTTP POST to /rpc. All requests and responses include a witVersion field set to '1'.

CallerWarning.title: CallerWarning
CallerWarning.description: Informs the lock acquirer that a symbol calling the newly locked symbol is itself locked by another session. The acquire still succeeds; warnings are informational.

lockedSymbol.description: Symbol path of the caller that holds an active lock

heldBy.description: Session ID holding the caller's lock

IntentOverlapItem.title: IntentOverlapItem
IntentOverlapItem.description: Another active intent overlaps the same file(s) and byte range.

overlappingSessionId.description: Session that declared the conflicting intent

LockIntersectionItem.title: LockIntersectionItem
LockIntersectionItem.description: A declared symbol path is locked by another session.

DepChainItem.title: DepChainItem
DepChainItem.description: A callee of a declared symbol is locked by another session.

ConflictItem.title: ConflictItem
ConflictItem.description: One detected conflict. Discriminated by the 'type' field.

ConflictReport.title: ConflictReport
ConflictReport.description: Returned by intent.declare. Summarizes all detected conflicts.

files.description: Comma-delimited file list with leading/trailing commas, e.g. ',src/auth.ts,src/api.ts,'

symbols.description: JSON array string of symbol names, e.g. '["validateToken"]'

startByte.description: Start byte offset of symbol range, or null for file-level intent

endByte.description: End byte offset of symbol range, or null for file-level intent

signature.description: Captured function signature (params + optional return type)

responderSessionId.description: Session that responded, or null if still proposed

respondedAt.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds, or null

Violation.title: Violation
Violation.description: A contract violation detected by check-contracts.

actual.description: The current signature in staged content, or '(symbol not found in staged content)'

data.description: Zod validation failure. The error object's data field contains flattened field errors.
heldBy (sessionId) and expiresAt (ISO 8601) included in data
current (status) and requested (status) included in data

methods.name: ping
methods.summary: Health check — verifies the daemon is reachable

methods.name: register
methods.summary: Register an agent with the daemon, associating a name with a session ID

params.name: name
params.description: Human-readable agent name

params.name: sessionId
params.description: Stable unique identifier for this agent session

agentId.description: Auto-incremented row ID assigned to this registration

methods.name: lock.acquire
methods.summary: Acquire an exclusive lock on a symbol with automatic TTL expiry
methods.description: Acquires an exclusive lock on a symbol path. If locked by a different active session, returns LOCK_CONFLICT. Same session refreshes TTL. Expired locks can be taken over. Builds caller-dependency graph on success and returns informational warnings for locked callers.

params.name: symbolPath
params.description: Symbol to lock. Format: 'file:symbolName' (e.g., 'src/auth.ts:validateToken'). Must contain ':'.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: Caller's session ID

params.name: ttlMs
params.description: Lock TTL in milliseconds. Default: 1800000 (30 minutes)

methods.name: lock.release
methods.summary: Release a lock held by the caller's session

params.name: symbolPath
params.description: Symbol path to release

params.name: sessionId
params.description: Must match the session that holds the lock

methods.name: lock.query
methods.summary: Query active (non-expired) locks, optionally filtered by session

params.name: sessionId
params.description: If provided, return only locks held by this session

methods.name: intent.declare
methods.summary: Declare intent to modify files/symbols and receive a conflict report
methods.description: Stores the intent and runs three conflict detectors: INTENT_OVERLAP (overlapping file/byte range), LOCK_INTERSECTION (declared symbol locked elsewhere), DEP_CHAIN (callee of declared symbol locked elsewhere). Always succeeds — conflicts are informational warnings in the response, not errors.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: Caller's session ID

params.name: description
params.description: Human-readable description of the intent

params.name: files
params.description: Array of relative file paths (at least one)

params.name: symbols
params.description: Symbol names (not paths) to associate with the intent. Used for byte-range resolution and conflict detection.

methods.name: intent.update
methods.summary: Update the status of an existing intent using forward-only transitions
methods.description: Valid transitions: declared -> active|resolved|abandoned, active -> resolved|abandoned. Terminal states (resolved, abandoned) have no outgoing transitions.

params.name: intentId
params.description: UUID of the intent to update

params.name: sessionId
params.description: Must match the session that declared the intent

params.name: status
params.description: Target status

methods.name: intent.query
methods.summary: Query intents with optional filters for session, file, and status
methods.description: Without a status filter, returns declared and active intents only. Providing an explicit status filter (including 'resolved' or 'abandoned') overrides the default. File filter uses exact segment matching.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: Filter by session ID

params.name: file
params.description: Filter by file path (exact segment match)

params.name: status
params.description: Filter by status. When absent, defaults to declared + active

methods.name: contract.propose
methods.summary: Propose a function signature contract for a symbol, capturing its current signature
methods.description: Reads the symbol's current signature from disk using tree-sitter (TypeScript/Python). Returns the captured signature as the contract's expected value. Another agent must call contract.respond to accept or reject. Supported languages: .ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx (TypeScript) and .py (Python).

params.name: sessionId
params.description: Proposing agent's session ID

params.name: symbolPath
params.description: Symbol whose signature to capture. Format: 'file:symbolName'. Must contain ':'.

signature.description: Captured function signature (params + optional return type)

methods.name: contract.respond
methods.summary: Accept or reject a proposed contract
methods.description: The responding session must differ from the proposing session (SELF_ACCEPT_NOT_ALLOWED). The contract must be in 'proposed' status.

params.name: contractId
params.description: UUID of the contract to respond to

params.name: sessionId
params.description: Responding agent's session ID (must differ from proposer)

params.name: accept
params.description: true to accept, false to reject

methods.name: contract.query
methods.summary: Query contracts, optionally filtered by symbol path and/or status

params.name: symbolPath
params.description: Filter by exact symbol path

params.name: status
params.description: Filter by status (proposed, accepted, rejected)

methods.name: check-contracts
methods.summary: Check staged file contents against accepted contracts for signature violations
methods.description: Used by the git pre-commit hook. The caller passes staged file content directly so the daemon never reads from disk. For each accepted contract whose symbol lives in a provided file, the daemon parses the staged content and compares signatures. The hook uses a 2-second timeout and fails open — if the daemon is unreachable, the commit proceeds.

params.name: files
params.description: Array of staged file entries

content.description: Full file content as a string

violations.description: List of contract violations. Empty array means no violations.
